title: Wrong number of type arguments
explanation: This error occurs when you provide the wrong number of generic type arguments to a type, function, or trait. Each generic parameter needs exactly one type argument unless it has a default.
error_message: error[E0107]: wrong number of type arguments: expected 1, found 0

fixes.description: Provide the correct number of type arguments
Let the compiler infer the types
fixes.code:
let x = vec![1, 2, 3]; // Inferred as Vec<i32>
let y: Result<_, String> = Ok(42); // Partial inference with _

tip: Common generic types: Vec<T> (1 arg), HashMap<K, V> (2 args), Result<T, E> (2 args), Option<T> (1 arg). Use _ to let the compiler infer a specific argument.
